title: Analysis: AI supercycle could drive semiconductor market beyond US$2 trillion by 2030
text:
Daniel Nenni
Founder
Staff member

The AI supercycle is reshaping semiconductor demand from a cyclical, consumer-led model into an infrastructure-driven growth phase. Digitimes’ view that the market could exceed US$2 trillion by 2030 reflects the rapid expansion of AI computing, where demand is no longer limited to CPUs and mobile SoCs but increasingly concentrated in GPUs, AI accelerators, HBM, advanced packaging, networking ASICs, and power management ICs.

The key technical driver is the scaling of data-center AI workloads. Training and inference of large language models require dense compute clusters with high memory bandwidth, low-latency interconnects, and advanced thermal management. This shifts value toward leading-edge foundry nodes, chiplet architectures, CoWoS-like advanced packaging, HBM stacks, and optical/electrical networking components. Research on AI supercomputers found that frontier AI system performance, hardware cost, and power requirements have been rising extremely quickly, reinforcing the scale of semiconductor demand behind AI infrastructure.

However, the path to a US$2 trillion semiconductor market depends on supply-side execution. Bottlenecks in advanced lithography, substrate capacity, HBM supply, and packaging throughput could constrain shipment growth even if demand remains strong. Recent market commentary also highlights advanced packaging and component mismatches as key constraints for AI chip supply chains.

Overall, the AI supercycle could structurally raise semiconductor content per server, per data center, and per watt of computing capacity. If enterprise AI adoption, sovereign AI projects, and hyperscaler capital expenditure continue expanding, semiconductors may become the core capital-good layer of the global digital economy by 2030.

Analysis: AI supercycle could drive semiconductor market beyond US$2 trillion by 2030

The global semiconductor market is entering a historically significant growth phase. According to WSTS's latest June forecast, global semiconductor revenue is projected to grow by nearly 90% in 2026, reaching approximately US$1.5 trillion. Growth is expected to remain exceptionally strong in...
